input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
# Install (adjust version as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent
# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact name
dotnet workload search maui
# Install MAUI (includes android, windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK dependencies

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically, but you may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find where Android SDK was provisioned
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# If not there, check:
dotnet android sdk info

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# List installed packages (discover versions)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Install a specific platform/build-tools if needed
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 4. Set up Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Check available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image and create AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Enable Hyper-V (requires admin + reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI targeting

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopment" /d 1

# The Windows App SDK comes with the maui-windows workload (already installed via step 2)
# Verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "windows"
```

## 6. Create and build a test project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Discover available target frameworks in the project
Select-String "TargetFrameworks" *.csproj

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
```

## 7. Run the app

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI &
dotnet run -f net8.0-android
```

---

**Key tips:**

- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you installed .NET 9 — discover with `dotnet --list-sdks`.
- If `dotnet workload install` fails behind a proxy, add `--source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json`.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- For Visual Studio integration, install the ".NET MAUI" component in the VS Installer — but the CLI steps above are sufficient for standalone builds.
